analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 9 finding(s) from 10 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: Top level instantiates SimDTM or dmi_jtag/SimJTAG and wires DMI requests/responses. dmi_jtag receives jtag_hash_i, okey_hash_i, ikey_hash_i and emits jtag_unlock_o. Debug-module master/slave signals exist, but the provided observations do not close reachability from debug-originated dm_master/dm_slave transactions into protected peripheral decode nor show address/range mediation.
findings.vulnerability_category: DMI request/response and debug module master/slave read/write signaling / debug module / potential downstream protected MMIO regions / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG/SimJTAG/DMI debug requester Operation: DMI request/response and debug module master/slave read/write signaling Object: debug module / potential downstream protected MMIO regions Expected guard: debug authentication/authorization such as jtag_unlock/hash authorization plus address/range mediation before debug-originated access reaches protected regions Observed behavior: Top level instantiates SimDTM or dmi_jtag/SimJTAG and wires DMI requests/responses. dmi_jtag receives jtag_hash_i, okey_hash_i, ikey_hash_i and emits jtag_unlock_o. Debug-module master/slave signals exist, but the provided observations do not close reachability from debug-originated dm_master/dm_slave transactions into protected peripheral decode nor show address/range mediation. Path: riscv_peripherals debug section: SimDTM/dmi_jtag/SimJTAG -> debug_req/debug_resp; dm_slave_* and dm_master_* signals declared nearby Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Impact and full path/reachability from debug master/slave signals to protected peripherals or memories were not provided. Address/range checks on debug-originated accesses were not observed. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-RISCV-DEBUG-001.

findings.summary: reglk_wrapper clears all exported lock state whenever jtag_unlock is asserted, so the debug unlock path can reset/clear lock bits rather than merely enabling an authenticated maintenance path; provided evidence does not show that jtag_unlock is only asserted after an authorization protocol strong enough to justify clearing sticky locks.
findings.vulnerability_category: clear/override lock state and write lock-control registers / reglk_mem/reglk_ctrl_o lock state consumed by protected wrappers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG/debug unlock path and AXI software writer Operation: clear/override lock state and write lock-control registers Object: reglk_mem/reglk_ctrl_o lock state consumed by protected wrappers Expected guard: jtag_unlock should only be asserted after authenticated JTAG/hash authorization and should not allow unauthenticated clearing/overriding of sticky lock bits; software writes should be gated by access-control and existing lock bits Observed behavior: reglk_wrapper assigns reglk_ctrl_o from reglk_mem. Its sequential block resets every reglk_mem entry to zero when ~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9), meaning assertion of jtag_unlock clears the lock registers. Normal writes are gated by en=en_acct&&acct_ctrl_i and selected reglk_ctrl_i bits, but jtag_unlock bypasses those per-register write guards through the reset/clear condition. Path: riscv_peripherals dmi_jtag.jtag_unlock_o -> top-level jtag_unlock -> i_reglk_wrapper.jtag_unlock -> reglk_mem clear -> reglk_ctrl_o -> protected wrapper reglk_ctrl_i slices Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Clearing lock bits can re-enable writes/readback to crypto, reset, accounting, packet/fuse-related and other protected registers that rely on reglk_ctrl_i, potentially bypassing intended sticky write-protection after software has locked the design.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The internals of dmi_jtag/hash challenge were not provided, so the strength of jtag_unlock authentication is uncertain. However, the lock-clear semantics on jtag_unlock are directly observed.

findings.summary: AES wrapper read paths expose key registers through MMIO read data paths under incomplete or inconsistent read gating; AES0 in particular assigns rdata from key0[address] before its case statement, leaving key-address reads outside explicit cases exposed when acct_ctrl_i permits the access.
findings.vulnerability_category: MMIO read/write/configure/start / AES key registers, plaintext/state/control registers, and ciphertext/result outputs / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI/NoC requester to AES wrappers Operation: MMIO read/write/configure/start Object: AES key registers, plaintext/state/control registers, and ciphertext/result outputs Expected guard: AES key/control/plaintext writes and reads of key/internal state should be mediated by acct_ctrl_i, reglk_ctrl_i, and debug_mode_i restrictions where applicable Observed behavior: AES wrappers gate local enable with en_acct && acct_ctrl_i and use reglk_ctrl_i bits on many writes/reads. However, AES0 read logic initializes rdata to key0[address[8:3]] whenever en is true before handling explicit cases, and default only forces zero if ct_valid. This creates a readback path for key0 entries at key-address indices not otherwise overwritten by the case. AES1 also has explicit key register readback controlled by key_we* address-decoder signals and reglk_ctrl_i[2]. Debug mode zeroizes some core key inputs, but does not itself remove all MMIO key readback paths. Path: NoC AXI-lite bridge -> aes0/aes1/aes2 wrapper axi_req_i -> axi_lite_interface -> en/we/address -> key/control/state registers -> rdata mux and AES core inputs Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An authorized-at-accounting-level but otherwise unprivileged or post-lock requester may read AES key material if lock bits are not correctly set or are cleared, compromising cryptographic confidentiality and enabling decryption/forgery of protected data.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Complete AES0/AES2 write cases and exact key address decoding are partially truncated; the role of key_we* as read-address selects in AES1 should be confirmed. Privilege/requester identity at the NoC port is not provided beyond acc_ctrl_c use.

findings.summary: HMAC wrapper gates local enable with en_acct && acct_ctrl_i. It has key, inner/outer hash byte arrays, key_hash_bypass, message data, and digest state. Debug mode zeroizes key and okey_hash inputs to the HMAC core but not ikey_hash in the shown assignments. Write observations show key/hash byte registers guarded by reglk_ctrl_i[5]. Read observations show message-data reads guarded by reglk_ctrl_i[2], but the provided snippet is truncated before key/hash/digest read cases are fully visible.
findings.vulnerability_category: MMIO read/write/start/bypass configure / HMAC key0, ikey_hash_bytes, okey_hash_bytes, key_hash_bypass, message data, digest/result, and control state / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI/NoC requester to HMAC wrapper Operation: MMIO read/write/start/bypass configure Object: HMAC key0, ikey_hash_bytes, okey_hash_bytes, key_hash_bypass, message data, digest/result, and control state Expected guard: HMAC key registers, bypass controls, and start/control registers should be protected by reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i; read data mux should not expose key or intermediate hash state except through authorized result paths Observed behavior: HMAC wrapper gates local enable with en_acct && acct_ctrl_i. It has key, inner/outer hash byte arrays, key_hash_bypass, message data, and digest state. Debug mode zeroizes key and okey_hash inputs to the HMAC core but not ikey_hash in the shown assignments. Write observations show key/hash byte registers guarded by reglk_ctrl_i[5]. Read observations show message-data reads guarded by reglk_ctrl_i[2], but the provided snippet is truncated before key/hash/digest read cases are fully visible. Path: NoC AXI-lite bridge -> hmac_wrapper axi_req_i -> axi_lite_interface -> key/data/hash/bypass registers -> hmac core and rdata mux Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full HMAC read mux, write cases for key_hash_bypass/start controls, and digest/key readback behavior are truncated. Impact cannot be asserted without knowing whether secret key/hash state is readable or bypass controls are insufficiently locked. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-RISCV-HMAC-001.

findings.summary: acct_wrapper gates register access with en_acct && acct_ctrl_i, initializes acct_mem entries to 0xffffffff on reset/rst_6, and uses reglk_ctrl bits to block writes to policy registers. acc_ctrl_o is composed from acct_mem words, with acct_mem[0] ORed with repeated we_flag. Top level constructs acc_ctrl_c indexed by priv_lvl_i and passes acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][j] into many wrappers, including reset, RNG, AES, SHA, HMAC, PKT, ACCT/REGLK, DMA and bootrom. This demonstrates enforcement hooks, but the authorization model for updating acct policy and the safety meaning of reset default 0xffffffff are not fully established.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure acc_ctrl policy and enforce reads/writes to protected peripherals / acc_ctrl_o policy bits and downstream peripheral acct_ctrl_i enforcement points / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI/NoC requester configuring or subject to access control Operation: configure acc_ctrl policy and enforce reads/writes to protected peripherals Object: acc_ctrl_o policy bits and downstream peripheral acct_ctrl_i enforcement points Expected guard: updates to access-control policy bits should require authorized software/privilege or existing lock mediation; acc_ctrl_o must be enforced consistently on protected request paths Observed behavior: acct_wrapper gates register access with en_acct && acct_ctrl_i, initializes acct_mem entries to 0xffffffff on reset/rst_6, and uses reglk_ctrl bits to block writes to policy registers. acc_ctrl_o is composed from acct_mem words, with acct_mem[0] ORed with repeated we_flag. Top level constructs acc_ctrl_c indexed by priv_lvl_i and passes acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][j] into many wrappers, including reset, RNG, AES, SHA, HMAC, PKT, ACCT/REGLK, DMA and bootrom. This demonstrates enforcement hooks, but the authorization model for updating acct policy and the safety meaning of reset default 0xffffffff are not fully established. Path: AXI/NoC request -> acct_wrapper -> acc_ctrl_o -> acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral_index] -> wrappers' acct_ctrl_i/en gating Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full acct_wrapper read/write map, exact top-level acct_wrapper instantiation, the semantics of 1 bits in acc_ctrl, and whether policy updates require privilege beyond current acc_ctrl_i were not provided. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-RISCV-ACCT-001.

findings.summary: Top-level parameters define distinct bases for Dm, ROM, CLINT, PLIC, AES0/AES1/AES2, SHA256, HMAC, PKT, ACCT, REGLK, DMA, RNG, RST, and RSA. The module exposes separate NoC ports and per-peripheral AXI-lite bridges. acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i] is passed to many wrappers. The only unique case observation provided is in PLIC adapter state handling, not a complete protected-region address decode or alias analysis.
findings.vulnerability_category: route MMIO read/write requests / peripheral address map including AES/HMAC/SHA/reglk/acct/reset/bootrom/fuse/debug/DMA regions / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: NoC/AXI requesters into riscv_peripherals Operation: route MMIO read/write requests Object: peripheral address map including AES/HMAC/SHA/reglk/acct/reset/bootrom/fuse/debug/DMA regions Expected guard: address-range decode should prevent unauthorized requesters from reaching protected regions, preserve privilege/debug/accounting attributes, and avoid unintended aliases/overlaps Observed behavior: Top-level parameters define distinct bases for Dm, ROM, CLINT, PLIC, AES0/AES1/AES2, SHA256, HMAC, PKT, ACCT, REGLK, DMA, RNG, RST, and RSA. The module exposes separate NoC ports and per-peripheral AXI-lite bridges. acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i] is passed to many wrappers. The only unique case observation provided is in PLIC adapter state handling, not a complete protected-region address decode or alias analysis. Path: top-level NoC ports and base address parameters -> per-peripheral noc_axilite_bridge instances -> protected wrappers Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Impact, complete decode/default cases, range comparisons, requester attribution, and alias/overlap checks were not provided. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-RISCV-DECODE-001.

findings.summary: riscv_peripherals instantiates rst_wrapper behind a NoC AXI-lite bridge and passes reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i selected by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][12], and debug_mode_i. The reset wrapper outputs rst_1..rst_13 including crypto and policy wrapper resets. Individual wrappers clear sensitive data/control state on their reset inputs in observed AES/SHA/HMAC snippets, while acct reset sets acct_mem to 0xffffffff and reglk reset clears reglk_mem to zero. The internal rst_wrapper authorization and reset register map are not provided.
findings.vulnerability_category: reset/clear protected state or policy / rst_1/rst_2/rst_3/rst_4/rst_6/rst_9/rst_11 and reset-cleared crypto/policy/lock state / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: reset controller requester or MMIO writer controlling resets Operation: reset/clear protected state or policy Object: rst_1/rst_2/rst_3/rst_4/rst_6/rst_9/rst_11 and reset-cleared crypto/policy/lock state Expected guard: software-triggered or external resets should be authorized and should not clear locks/policy into unsafe defaults; secrets should be cleared Observed behavior: riscv_peripherals instantiates rst_wrapper behind a NoC AXI-lite bridge and passes reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i selected by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][12], and debug_mode_i. The reset wrapper outputs rst_1..rst_13 including crypto and policy wrapper resets. Individual wrappers clear sensitive data/control state on their reset inputs in observed AES/SHA/HMAC snippets, while acct reset sets acct_mem to 0xffffffff and reglk reset clears reglk_mem to zero. The internal rst_wrapper authorization and reset register map are not provided. Path: buf_ariane_rst_noc2 -> i_rst_axilite_bridge -> rst_axi_req -> i_rst_wrapper -> rst_1/rst_2/rst_3/rst_4/rst_6/rst_9/rst_11 -> protected wrappers Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: rst_wrapper internals are absent, so authorization of reset writes and whether defaults are safe cannot be concluded. The meaning of acct_mem=0xffffffff is not specified. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-RISCV-RESET-001.

findings.summary: Top-level declares fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata and connects them to pkt_wrapper. pkt_wrapper also receives reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i selected from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5]. The PKT NoC AXI-lite bridge exposes pkt_wrapper to the NoC. Provided observations do not show pkt_wrapper internals, fuse memory implementation, or whether fuse_rdata is returned on the MMIO read mux.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/expose fuse data or use fuse-derived policy / fuse_req, fuse_addr, fuse_rdata and any fuse-backed configuration/secrets / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: MMIO/debug requester or internal fuse consumer Operation: read/expose fuse data or use fuse-derived policy Object: fuse_req, fuse_addr, fuse_rdata and any fuse-backed configuration/secrets Expected guard: fuse reads should be limited to authorized logic and not exposed through general MMIO/debug read muxes without privilege/debug authorization and address checks Observed behavior: Top-level declares fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata and connects them to pkt_wrapper. pkt_wrapper also receives reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i selected from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5]. The PKT NoC AXI-lite bridge exposes pkt_wrapper to the NoC. Provided observations do not show pkt_wrapper internals, fuse memory implementation, or whether fuse_rdata is returned on the MMIO read mux. Path: NoC PKT port -> pkt_axilite_bridge -> pkt_wrapper -> fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Impact, pkt_wrapper read/write behavior, fuse_rdata routing, debug exposure, and external fuse block behavior were not provided. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-RISCV-FUSE-001.

findings.summary: Top-level defines DMABase and exposes buf_ariane_dma_noc2/noc3 ports. Search observations show a dma_wrapper instance behind a DMA AXI-lite bridge with acct_ctrl_i from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][8]. The evidence indicates a DMA control peripheral is itself gated, but does not show DMA master transactions into other protected MMIO regions or whether requester identity/permissions are preserved for DMA-originated fabric accesses.
findings.vulnerability_category: DMA read/write through NoC/fabric / protected MMIO regions reachable from DMA NoC interface / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: DMA NoC requester Operation: DMA read/write through NoC/fabric Object: protected MMIO regions reachable from DMA NoC interface Expected guard: DMA-originated transactions should be subject to access-control/range/PMP/accounting restrictions and should not update protected crypto/reglk/acct/reset/fuse registers unless authorized Observed behavior: Top-level defines DMABase and exposes buf_ariane_dma_noc2/noc3 ports. Search observations show a dma_wrapper instance behind a DMA AXI-lite bridge with acct_ctrl_i from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][8]. The evidence indicates a DMA control peripheral is itself gated, but does not show DMA master transactions into other protected MMIO regions or whether requester identity/permissions are preserved for DMA-originated fabric accesses. Path: buf_ariane_dma_noc2/noc3 -> i_dma_axilite_bridge -> dma_axi_req/resp -> i_dma_wrapper; potential downstream DMA master path not observed Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Impact, dma_wrapper internals, any DMA master egress path, requester identity propagation, and reachability to protected MMIO regions were not provided. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-RISCV-DMA-001.
